suggestions.title: Stats Bar Grid Overflows Beyond 5 Columns
suggestions.description: The stats bar uses `grid-template-columns: repeat(5, 1fr)` but dynamically adds error and stuck worker cards beyond those 5 slots. When both an error card AND a stuck card appear simultaneously, you get 7 cells in a 5-col grid — the overflow wraps to a second row, creating an uneven orphaned row. Switch to `repeat(auto-fill, minmax(160px, 1fr))` or explicitly reserve space for the optional cards.

suggestions.title: Office Floor Is Unusable on Mobile
suggestions.description: On 375px mobile, worker names and desk labels in the Office view are rendered at 0.32–0.38rem — effectively sub-pixel and unreadable. Characters overlap or stack awkwardly. The isometric floor layout is a desktop-first experience and needs either: (a) a minimum character/label scale enforced via CSS clamp(), (b) a simplified 2-column grid list view as a mobile fallback, or (c) a pinch-to-zoom container. This is the single biggest UX gap on mobile.

suggestions.title: Inter Font Never Loaded — Silently Falls Back
suggestions.description: index.css declares `font-family: 'Inter', 'Segoe UI', system-ui` but there is no @import or <link> tag loading Inter from Google Fonts or any self-hosted source. Users without Inter pre-installed (most non-macOS users) silently render in Segoe UI or the system font, breaking typographic consistency. Fix with a preconnect + preload Google Fonts link in index.html, or bundle Inter via npm (fontsource-inter).

suggestions.title: App.jsx and Office.jsx Are Too Large to Maintain
suggestions.description: App.jsx is 1,273 lines and Office.jsx is 1,417 lines. App.jsx alone contains StatsBar, ActivityLog, WorkerCard, ProgressBar, DeltaBadge, VibeSparkline, ShortcutToast, LoadingSkeleton, EmptyState, and the main App component. Extract each logical component into its own file under src/components/. This is not cosmetic — it directly impacts debuggability, test coverage, and future onboarding speed.

suggestions.title: completionToast Rendered with Inline Styles — Breaks Light Mode
suggestions.description: The worker completion toast (🎉 X shipped it!) uses hardcoded inline styles (`background: 'var(--surface2)'`, `color: 'var(--text)'`, `boxShadow` with hardcoded rgba values). While CSS variables work in inline styles, the border and glow color are passed as a raw hex prop making the component impossible to theme or override. Move to a `.completion-toast` CSS class with a CSS custom property for the accent color, consistent with every other notification element.

suggestions.title: Escape Key Does Not Clear Active Tag Filter
suggestions.description: Keyboard shortcut: Escape currently only calls `document.activeElement?.blur()`. When a task tag filter is active (selectedTag is set), pressing Escape should also clear it — this is the expected behavior in any filter UI. A user using keyboard-only navigation has no way to clear the filter without clicking or using mouse. Add `setSelectedTag(null)` to the Escape handler in the global keydown effect.

suggestions.title: No aria-live Announcement When Data Syncs
suggestions.description: When the polling sync completes, worker counts and statuses update silently for screen readers. Add a visually-hidden `aria-live='polite'` region (sr-only) that announces something like 'Dashboard updated, 3 workers active' after each successful sync. The isSyncing state and lastSynced timestamp are already tracked — just pipe the summary string into the live region. Low effort, high accessibility win.

suggestions.title: Activity Log Worker Filter Excludes Non-Roster Workers
suggestions.description: The worker filter pill bar below the activity log only renders buttons for workers in the `roster` array from workers.json. Workers who appear in activity.json events but are NOT in the roster (e.g. temporary workers, removed workers, or Bloberto himself for system events) cannot be filtered. Either derive filter options from the union of roster + unique worker names seen in activityLog, or add a fallback showing all workers mentioned in the log.

suggestions.title: Dead CSS Block at Bottom of App.css
suggestions.description: A block labeled `/* === Mobile Header Fix (Luna Review #41) === */` at the very end of App.css targets `.tab-content`, `.header-subtitle`, and `.tab-toggle` — none of which exist as class names in the JSX. These are ghost selectors from an old design iteration that were never cleaned up. Remove the dead block (~14 lines). Also check Office.css for similar orphaned selectors from past refactors.

suggestions.title: getTaskTags() Called Twice Per Worker Per Render
suggestions.description: In the workers grid, `getTaskTags(w.task)` is called both inside WorkerCard (for rendering tags) and at the call site as `!getTaskTags(w.task).some(...)` to compute the `isDimmed` prop. This doubles the regex matching work per worker. Either memoize tags inside WorkerCard with useMemo, compute isDimmed inside WorkerCard using already-computed tags, or pass the tags array as a prop instead of the raw task string.

suggestions.title: Conference Table 'Last Huddle' Text Is Sub-Pixel
suggestions.description: The 'Last huddle' timestamp below the conference table is rendered at `font-size: 0.38rem` — at 16px base that is approximately 6px, smaller than most screens can render crisply. It is effectively invisible. Either bump it to a minimum of 0.65rem and style it as a proper soft label, or remove the feature entirely. Invisible UI is worse than no UI.

suggestions.title: Vibe Sparkline Has No Legend or Context
suggestions.description: The vibe sparkline in the Team Vibe stat card is charming but completely opaque to new users. The y-axis represents vibe rank (after-hours → crushing), the x-axis is time, and dot colors map to vibe states — but none of this is documented. Add a tooltip on hover for the SVG element explaining the axes, or add aria-description text. The existing `<title>` tags on individual circles are good but the overall chart needs context.

suggestions.title: Add Worker Name Search to Dashboard Grid
suggestions.description: As the roster grows, a text search input above the workers grid would help. A simple uncontrolled input filtering `activeWorkers` by `worker.name.toLowerCase().includes(query)` would cover 90% of the use case with minimal code. Consider placing it in the section header next to 'Active Workers (N)'.

suggestions.title: Version String Still Shows v1.0.0-chaos
suggestions.description: The footer version is unchanged through at least 27 reviews. Given the substantial feature additions (vibe history, sparklines, custom SVG characters, stuck worker beacon, activity log filters, hash routing, etc.), consider adopting date-based versioning or at least bumping to v2.0.0-chaos to communicate the scale of changes. It's a cosmetic issue but it signals 'nothing has changed' to returning users.

summary: Review #27 — major leap forward from the previous 3.6. The codebase has matured significantly: intelligent polling (visibility-aware + idle-pause), a genuinely delightful vibe streak system with sparkline history, click-to-focus cross-tab worker navigation, task tag filtering with dimming, stuck worker beacon, per-worker completion confetti, and rich custom SVG character avatars that include error-state PANIC screens. The Office view is a legitimate showpiece — conference table vibe behavior, time-of-day sky gradients, away signs, ghost/OOO workers, and Bloberto's ambient speech bubbles are all cohesive and polished. Accessibility has meaningfully improved (skip link, full ARIA tablist pattern, reduced-motion throughout, coarse-pointer detection). Three issues need fixing before this hits prime time: the stats bar grid silently breaks with 7+ cards, the office view is genuinely unusable on mobile at 375px, and Inter font is declared but never loaded. Code health is the main ongoing debt — App.jsx at 1273 lines and Office.jsx at 1417 lines are becoming a liability. Fun factor is off the charts. This is the best version yet.
